title: Tyre, exhaust and windscreen fitters
description: Install glass in windows, skylights, store fronts, and display cases, or on surfaces, such as building fronts, interior walls, ceilings, and tabletops. Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of mechanics, installers, and repairers. May also advise customers on recommended services. Excludes team or work leaders. Repair and refinish automotive vehicle bodies and straighten vehicle frames. (Based on 11 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Cut and attach mounting strips, moldings, gaskets, or clips in preparation for glass installation.
Load and arrange glass or mirrors onto delivery trucks, using suction cups or cranes to lift glass.
Install glass doors into frames, securing them with hinges, handles, locks, or other hardware.
Cut, assemble, fit, or attach metal-framed glass enclosures for structures such as showers, display cases, or skylights.
Drive trucks to installation sites and unload mirrors, glass equipment, or tools as needed.
Install pre-assembled frameworks for windows or doors using hand tools for glass panels.
Measure and mark outlines on glass for cutting, then score with cutters' wheels and break as needed.
Grind or polish glass to smooth edges and prepare for installation.
Prepare glass for cutting by resting it on racks and applying a thin oil layer along cutting lines.
Secure mirrors in position using mastic cement, putty, bolts, or screws to attach them properly.
Fasten glass panes into sashes or frames, adding seals or putty around edges to ensure proper security.
Operate cranes or hoists with suction cups to lift large pieces of glass during installation.
Install or repair replacement glass and ensure proper fitting in vehicles or structures.
Inspect windows and doors for proper functioning, making adjustments or repairs as necessary.
Remove damaged glass sections before installing new glass or reinforcements where needed.
Confer with customers to determine project requirements, provide cost estimates, and discuss work to be performed.
Order replacements for glass materials or parts as required for upcoming projects.
Maintain cleanliness of the work area, ensuring tools and materials are organized and accessible.
Inspect glass panels for defects or irregularities before installation to ensure quality control.
Seal punctures in tubeless tires by inserting adhesive material and expanding rubber plugs into punctures, using hand tools.
Locate punctures in tubeless tires by visual inspection or using water baths to observe air bubbles.
Raise vehicles using hydraulic jacks and remount wheels onto vehicles.
Inspect tire casings for defects, such as holes or tears, and inflate inner tubes to locate leaks.
Reassemble tires onto wheels and rotate tires to different positions on vehicles, using hand tools.
Repair or replace valve stems and remove puncturing objects from tires.
Remove broken or damaged glass windshields from vehicles using hand tools, and install, repair, or replace safety glass and related materials.
Patch tubes with adhesive rubber patches or seal rubber patches to tubes using hot vulcanizing plates.
Prepare rims and wheel drums for reassembly by scraping, grinding, or sandblasting them as needed.
Replace or adjust motorized or manual window-raising mechanisms and reinstall clips, moldings, or windshield wipers after glass replacement.
Remove cylinder heads and grind valves to replace defective parts using hand and power tools.
Apply heat to plastic panels to reshape them using hot-air welding guns or immersion in hot water.
Check and remove moisture or contamination in damaged areas until repairs are complete.
Drive service trucks to industrial sites to provide services or respond to emergency calls.
Record repairs made, time spent, and parts used for documentation purposes.
